content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 9

   - If LFF for VN can > 50%, what is stopping us from doing that for the
   other markets?

Comparatively, markets like SG/PH/TH feels like there is
   still room to grow



Slide 12

   - For these rejections, how do they hope to make it up in terms of
   assortment, NPC and LFF?

- Given that TT now operates cross border, how are these brands
   managing?
Do they operate with 2 shops via distributor?

Slide 13

   - For those in A, do we have local distributors name?

How does the local
   distributors do vs CB?
And vs local distributors in TT?

Slide 15

   - Torridien is not good?
And we still have so little matched SKU?

And
   big assortment gap?
- Cosrx assortment gap is big as well?

Slide 17

   - Seems like situation in TH is not good in general?

Have we started to
   negotiate yet?
Am not sure price differential is all due to TT rebates?

Slide 23

   - Can show 2025 numbers and the growth % and P&L impact for each
   scenario?
- Why do we not want to go for Aggressive #1?

The difference is just +2
   HC and more agency right?
- What does subsidy slot x2 mean?

Slide 24

   - Can show me the latest (Nov) Gross TR% for KR and JP?

Broken down into
      - Mall (KR/JP)
      - Non-Mall (KR/JP)
   - How are we thinking about TR increase for 2026?
- Will we be increasing commissions for KR?

Will it come from Mall or
      MP?
Will it come from compulsory paid ads?

What is the median paid ads
      right now?

And if we look at SMT 1 -3, how many sellers fall below the
      median?
- For JP, how does the MP commissions compare to KR MP?

Should we
      increase commissions on top of compulsory paid ads?

Why did we arrive at
      2%?
What is the median paid ads right now?

Hi Ian

Following up with JP’s ‘26 topline target and PnL simulation by scenario
and please refer to below for summary. For detail can refer to the
attached. Let me know if any questions. Thanks.


   1.

   ‘26 Dec ADO target 25k('26 full year vs. '25 full year YoY 63% vs. PLT’s
   YoY 27% which is also the original case scenario) with more "systematic"
   key growth drivers - new sellers/new sku/ads program. (note: If hiring
   on track, will review by end of ‘26 Q1 to aim for aggressive scenario of
   ADO 30k by ‘26 Dec as we discussed)
   2.

   Both commission and compulsory ads programs will contribute to
   increasing overall JP TR% (also considering JP have ~3% gap vs. competitors
   for commission) to 14.12% and gross ads TR% up to 2.7%(vs. ‘25 avg of
   1.5%)


   -

   Base commission increase of 1*%** for all markets(TH increase 2%
   following local)* given that JP non mall X Beauty comms TR% is lower vs
   local by 1~4% in MY/TH/VN/PH(biggest gap in TH by 4%, thus higher increase
   in commission) while JP is higher in SG/TW vs. local
   -

   Compulsory ads program of 2% for all markets*(excl TW/BR following
   local)* as the median of JP sellers(with ~50% of GMV)’ avg ads TR% is
   1.3% and sense this is still within manageable in terms of seller sentiment.

(note: will monitor seller reaction and review again in beginning of H2 if
additional increase in ads program is feasible)

Please refer to attached for the TR comparison for KRJP vs. CN/local for
mall/non-mall Beauty category thanks to Faye team.
Based on CN/local benchmark and to improve KRJP negative PC2 in certain
markets, in general Faye propose to increase commission for both KRJP in
line with local and also introduce ads compulsory program.

commission increase for all markets 1%(TH 2%, VN TXN, VCN follow local) and
ads compulsory program of 2% for all markets(excl TW/BR following local).
And for KR, will revert by tmr on the commission and ads compulsory program
% after reflecting changes in '26 topline and if needed will apply from Feb
rather than Jan. Let me know if any questions. Thanks

Thanks.



Slide 3

   - I do not quite understand the framework in general
   - What exactly are we benchmarking for Mall + Beauty and Non-Mall +
   Beauty that governs our commissions hike recommendation?

It feels quite
   random right now, and I do not see any pattern



For eg.

   - For Mall sellers, if they already spending so much in SG and MY vs
   CNCB + Local, why would we tax them so much more?

There is a CODB at the
   end of the day
   - For TH and VN, there is still quite a bit of room to grow when
   compared to CNCB and SG/MY, so we should charge them more?
*‘26 Dec ADO target 25k('26 full year vs. '25 full year YoY 63% vs.
Both* commission and compulsory ads programs will contribute to

content.answer:
For KR managed malls, benchmark is their peer group, meaning we make
   managed brands to spend at least to their peer group’s medianTR which is
   30%. Then on average these sellers will need to increase TR by <1% overall
   so this seems doable. How we do it : we give sellers this peer group median
   TR target, give them one quarter to achieve this TR, and if they fail to do
   so then the next quarter we’ll deduct the gap TR% from their escrow. We
   already ran this concept with UST brands during T2T meetings for the past
   few weeks and their main questions were “are our peer brands spending this
   much? Can we get this data for internal approval? Can you also take into
   account our offline & offsite spending.” So I think this is a
   difficult-but-feasible and good way to motivate sellers’ spending for SHP
   conversion and guarantee certain TR%

Why we benchmark peer group is because this is how most KR malls
      actually strategize, as Korean brands have similar branding and are
      competitors to each other

Why we don’t benchmark CODB of TTS is because out of the 11 UST
      managed brands brands I had T2T meetings with this year-end, all except 1
      brand’s plan next year is to cap or shrink TTS GMV due to its thin margin
      and 2 brand among them even closing TTS shop due to margin, so
it’s not the
      proper benchmark for for KR

For KR unmanaged brands/resellers, the benchmark is also their peer
   group. KR reseller average paid ads TR is 2.2% so making these sellers do
   mandatory ads at 2%. Unlike managed mall, cannot apply median because
   median TR is 0% and irrelevant. How we do it : we use the mandatory ads
   program to uplift the topline using it as tool to push for reseller SIP
   adoption as well (TR increasing due to mandatory ads so more pushed to do
   SIP and not pay the non-SIP penalty)

-> Having said that, with systematic programs in place for ‘26, KR can
achieve “even better” MP PC2 + SLS PnL $ vs. the scenario if we follow the
commission increase of CNCB, and below is the proposal with its impact for
your reference. In the very worst case if Legal team says such escrow
deduct for managed malls’ TR% program and mandatory ads program are not
doable in Korean regulatory environment, then we’ll re-propose with
commissions

[image: image.png]

-> Summary: KR proposes NOT to increase base commission but run
KR-customized TR improvement and ads program and aim KR ‘26 topline to
reach yearly 82k ADO (+40% YoY vs. 2025,  which is +20% YoY higher than the
commission increase only scenario). In terms of MP PC2 + SLS PnL, it will
be increased by $11.8M(vs ‘25) reaching yearly $35.6mil which Program only
case will generate “more” PnL of $0.9mil(+2.51% increase) vs. commission
only case. Also overall TR% reaching 18.97% and PC2% 2.59%(overall PnL %
6.17%)

[image: image.png]

================================================================================================

 For Non-Mall + Beauty, across most markets, there is probably room to
close the gap vs CNCB. The question is how to do it.

 I need to understand what the framework governing the thinking works first

   -

   Then I need a solution for KR Mall, KR Non-Mall, JP Non-Mall.

Do you think we can go with +2% in commissions and +2% in compulsory ads
take rate for JPCB?

-> For

Competitor CODB - for JP sellers in other CB EC PLT is known to be
   roughly 3%p higher vs. SHP

Hi Ian,

Pls find the updated version attached with the structure mentioned in the
email loop

   - KR: What target should we set? and how to achieve the target? Options
   are given and Discussion Required in deck P5/7/8
   - JP: given size very small and ABS very high vs all other BUs, numbers
   are screwed, to use proposed 2% commission increase + 2% compulsory to
   simulate impact, but added backup of same method as KR

Best,

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 5

   - Not quite sure why ABS is a factor here
   - We should compare KR Mall with CN mall and KR MP with CN MP



Slide 7

   - Quite confused here on what we are trying to show
   - If we look at Slide 3, for KR Mall, we should charge Ads Growth Plan
   to catchup. That would make up the difference in TH/VN/PH. And possible to
   charge across all markets, for sellers > the %, they will not be affected
   anyway.
   - For MP, we can probably charge both Ads and Comms to catchup



Slide 8

   - For FBS, what is the breakdown of Mall vs MP ADO right now?

Slide 14

   - Think this is OK?
- Why is there no impact in TW?

   For MP, we can probably charge both Ads and Comms to catchup
